title: Intriguingly Sour Peruvian Rice Tacu Tacu
description: A sour, fluffy, and cozy Peruvian dish featuring golden syrup and lardons, rendered to perfection in a Korean brass bowl
instructions: Heat the lardons in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy and golden brown
Remove the lardons from the skillet with a slotted spoon and set aside on a paper towel lined plate
Add the diced aji amarillo pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute
Add the chopped scallions to the skillet and cook for an additional minute
Add the rinsed and drained rice to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly
Add the low sodium chicken broth, golden syrup, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender
Stir in the fresh lime juice and crispy lardons
Transfer the Tacu Tacu to a Korean brass bowl and serve immediately
